channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Judge clears way for Trump’s plan to downsize federal workforce with deferred resignation program
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday cleared the way for President Donald Trump ’s plan to downsize the federal workforce with a deferred resignation program. It was a significant legal victory for the Republican president after a string of courtroom setbacks.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Attorney General Pam Bondi rails against New York leaders as she announces immigration lawsuit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: BREAKING: Active shooter on Archer City Golf Course
item.description: Little information is known at this time, but we know it started around 4:30 p.m. at the Archer City Country Club golf course.
item.dc:creator: Angie Lankford, NewsNation

item.title: White House says it has the right to punish AP reporters over Gulf naming dispute
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House said Wednesday that news organizations that refuse to use President Donald Trump’s new name for the Gulf of Mexico were telling “lies” and insisted it would continue to bar Associated Press journalists from presidential events.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Early look: The views and venues of the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: The 2026 Winter Games will bring the world's best athletes to Northern Italy, with Milan and Cortina as key host cities, marking the first time in 20 years that the games return to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Orange, KREX 5

item.title: Texas county approves holding election to make SpaceX's Starbase its own city
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — A Texas county on Wednesday approved holding an election sought by SpaceX that would let residents living around billionaire Elon Musk 's company decide whether to formally create a new city called Starbase.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: List: The more than 500 JOANN stores expected to close amid second bankruptcy filing
item.description: When JOANN filed for bankruptcy last month, the company said it expected all of its stores to remain open.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Former longtime Illinois legislative leader Michael Madigan is convicted in corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A Chicago Democrat who once set much of Illinois’ political agenda as the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history was convicted of conspiracy, bribery and wire fraud Wednesday in a split verdict in his high-profile corruption trial.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Eastern storm cuts power to tens of thousands as California braces for flooding
item.description: Storms dumped heavy snow and freezing rain on a swath of the U.S. East from Kentucky to the nation's capital, causing hundreds of traffic accidents, knocking out power in places and threatening to flood waterways as temperatures began rising Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: House Republicans unveil blueprint to extend $4.5 trillion in tax cuts and lift the debt ceiling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans released a budget plan Wednesday that sets the stage for advancing many of President Donald Trump's top domestic priorities, providing for up to $4.5 trillion in tax cuts and a $4 trillion increase in the debt limit so that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California farm donates hundreds of thousands of eggs to wildfire victims and first responders
item.description: A California farm is donating hundreds of thousands of fresh eggs to people affected by last month’s devastating wildfires in the Los Angeles Area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The relationship between the White House and its press corps is time-tested
item.description: When the Trump administration barred Associated Press reporters from presidential events, it did so after an or-else demand that the news agency change its style from the “Gulf of Mexico” to the “Gulf of America,” in line with the president's executive order.
item.dc:creator: Laurie Kellman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: John Legend says family is considering leaving California after wildfires
item.description: Last month's devastating wildfires may prompt John Legend and his wife, Chrissy Teigen, the latest celebrities to flee California.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Valentine’s Day scams to watch for this week
item.description: As Valentine's Day approaches, scammers are looking to deceive people. Here are the top scams to be aware of, and how to avoid falling for them.
item.dc:creator: Zach Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Son of accused attempted Trump assassin has outburst in court after pleading guilty to serious charges
item.description: The son of a man accused of trying to kill then-presidential candidate Donald Trump will be sentenced in May for possessing child porn materials, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dog seen riding in unsecured kennel along Indiana expressway now recovering
item.description: Video showed the dog in a kennel on a trailer that was being towed along an Indiana expressway in frigid temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pilot killed in collision involving Mötley Crüe singer's plane identified as Marine Corps veteran
item.description: Joie Vitosky, 78, was certified as a flight engineer since 1978 and a pilot since 2019, according to FAA records.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Barker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Egg prices hit a record high as the U.S. contends with an ongoing bird flu outbreak, but consumers didn't need government figures released Wednesday to tell them eggs are terribly expensive and hard to find at times because of an ongoing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Your closed Party City may soon turn into one of these familiar stores
item.description: What used to be your local Party City store may soon be selling books, recliner chairs, cowboy boots or discount merchandise.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: 2 homeless children freeze to death while sleeping in van with family in Detroit: police
item.description: Police in Detroit said that two homeless children froze to death while they slept in a van with their family on Monday, while the city’s mayor revealed that their mother had reached out to homeless services a few months ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Audio from Titan submersible implosion released by NOAA
item.description: In the 23-second recording, the sound of static can be heard, followed by a booming noise — then more static.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, Fox 5

item.title: Christian aid groups weigh life-threatening choices about who to help after USAID funding pause
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a warehouse in Haiti, nearly four metric tons of seeds cannot be distributed. Soon the planting season will be gone and with it, the best chance for those seeds to produce emergency food.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Monty the giant schnauzer wins Westminster dog show
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — This time, the giant schnauzer went the full Monty. After coming close in the last two years, Monty the giant schnauzer won the top prize at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show Tuesday night, leaving handler and co-owner Katie Bernardin almost too emotional to speak.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AP says reporter blocked from White House over ‘Gulf of Mexico’ policy
item.description: The barring of the journalist, the AP said in a statement, was in connection with AP's refusal to "align its editorial standards with President Donald Trump’s executive order renaming the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Disney, amid changes to DEI initiatives, will alter content warning ahead of 'Peter Pan,' 'Dumbo' and other older films
item.description: Amid the changes, Disney is also replacing the language in the content advisory disclaimers that auto-play before certain older films streaming on Disney+.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: New York City's mayor has 4 months to persuade Democratic voters he's not Trump's puppet
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams declared Tuesday that he is “no longer facing legal questions” after the Justice Department moved to shield him from the bribery charges that have been hanging over his reelection campaign.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disney, amid changes to DEI initiatives, will alter content warning ahead of 'Peter Pan,' 'Dumbo' and other older films
item.description: Disney has become the latest company to change its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives as pressure from the federal government to eliminate such programs continues.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Another massive grocery chain is limiting egg sales
item.description: Eggs have been hard to come by for some consumers, with empty shelves spotted in some stores dating back to late last year. At the time, Trader Joe’s joked the egg shortage was prompted by chickens being on strike.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: No, your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE illegally accessed your data
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos claim otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PBS closes DEI office to comply with executive order
item.description: PBS' move comes as Trump's FCC also continues to turn up the heat on public broadcasters over taxpayer funding and editorial direction.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Crush of Valentine's Day flowers: Nearly a billion go through this city to you
item.description: If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration moves to shut down Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau is likely the next federal agency on the chopping block as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk continue the DOGE agenda to shrink the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Catalytic converters stolen from Illinois district's school buses, forcing students to stay home
item.description: A suburban Illinois school district was forced to hold an emergency e-learning day after the catalytic converters were stolen from "over 80%" of its buses.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, Fox 5

item.title: The remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said. The Nome Volunteer Fire Department made the announcement on its Facebook page Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4
